title: Record Schemas, Field Provenance, Confidence, and Data Quality · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs record schemas, field provenance, confidence, and data quality from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for loss-aware versioned site record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.
Attach source URL, response identity, node or attribute path, raw value digest, transform version, time, and decision to each field.
Use confidence only when tied to observable rule evidence, calibration, thresholds, and review consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
